item.title: How to Find Focus When It’s Most Elusive
item.description: Concentrating on creative work requires setting limits.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: They Tried to Move a Whale
item.description: A juvenile humpback washed ashore in Oregon. Could local residents save it?
item.dc:creator: Robin Romm

item.title: Your Next Dog May Live Longer
item.description: A new pill could soon extend dogs’ lives. How will that change our relationship with our pets?
item.dc:creator: Ross Andersen

item.title: Deepfakes Are Coming for Your Bank Account
item.description: OpenAI made the perfect tool for scammers.
item.dc:creator: Lila Shroff

item.title: Another Reason the White House Ballroom Is an Eyesore
item.description: Trump’s pet project would contort a White House design that is supposed to emphasize democracy and openness.
item.dc:creator: Neil Flanagan

item.title: My AI Matchmaker Let Me Down
item.description: Is this the future of online dating?
item.dc:creator: Annie Joy Williams

item.title: For a Time, the U.S. Protected Democracy
item.description: A requiem for the Voting Rights Act
item.dc:creator: Vann R. Newkirk II

item.title: Congress Can’t Meet Its Own Iran-War Deadline
item.description: As the war reaches its 60th day, lawmakers appear unwilling or unable to do anything.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Atlantic Reads:How to Be a DissidentWith Gal Beckerman
item.description: TheAtlanticstaff writer Gal Beckerman and podcast host Adam Harris discuss Beckerman’s new book,How to Be a Dissident.

item.title: Animal FarmIs Not for Kids but They Made It a Kids’ Movie Anyway
item.description: There are many more animated children’s films to be made based on dystopian source material.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: An Unexpected Type of Beach Read
item.description: Take pleasure in the mismatch between dangerous tales and leisurely environs.
item.dc:creator: Emma Sarappo

item.title: Did a Human Write This?
item.description: The tool that knows if you used ChatGPT
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: The Psychiatrist’s Case for Downsizing a Friendship
item.description: A new book on attachment theory proposes a radical solution for the anxious among us.
item.dc:creator: Faith Hill

item.title: The Real Reason Iran Hasn’t Struck a Deal
item.description: The standoff isn’t about hard-liners blocking pragmatists inside Iran, but about both sides believing that they have won the war.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Wright

item.title: Photos of the Week: Screeching Championship, Direct Democracy, Victory Plunge
item.description: Parkour practice in the mountains of Afghanistan, an Anzac Day parade in Australia, a lunar-landing art project in Northern Ireland, corgi racing in the Czech Republic, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: A New World Record
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: So, About That AI Bubble
item.description: Thanks to the rise of Claude Code and other AI agents, revenues are finally catching up to the hype.
item.dc:creator: Rogé Karma

item.title: Iran’s Leaders Mostly Want a Deal
item.description: Sure, there’s “infighting,” but not along the lines many assume.
item.dc:creator: Arash Azizi

item.title: The Mysterious Obsession With Obama’s Fake Son
item.description: Would he look like Trayvon Martin?
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The Secret to Success Is ‘Monotasking’
item.description: In a world full of distractions, getting your brain to focus on one thing at a time requires radical measures.
item.dc:creator: David Epstein
